_schema: one entry per pipeline decision: {knob, value, basis: token|fraction|eval|structural, evidence, alternatives, status}. TODO item 2: every decision states WHY, quantitatively. status=justified means evidence recorded below; status=pending-evidence means the knob still needs its sweep.

decisions.evidence: static-score sweep on RDPro/beast (full tree, 820 raw names): threshold 3 -> 533, 4 -> 258, 5 -> 205, 6 -> 194, 7 -> 102 selected. 5 sits at the start of the flat shelf (5->6 drops only 11 names) while 4 admits +53 weakly-evidenced names and 7 halves the surface.
decisions.status: justified-by-counts; comprehension pass-rate per threshold still pending (needs OPENAI_API_KEY run)

decisions.evidence: a name defined at many sites is a trait/interface method implemented per class, not one operation. RDPro: run x29, write x27, close x27, read x24, getPixelValue x21, componentType x18, isEmpty x16. With the penalty, 13 names drop from the selected set - all interface plumbing - while all 12 demo-critical APIs (raptorJoin, raptorJoinFeature, geoTiff, shapefile, zonalStatsLocal, spatialJoin, overlay, saveAsGeoTiff, reshapeNN, retile, filterPixels, mapPixels) stay selected. getPixelValue: 17/17 error_log entries are status=fail (a failure magnet, never a verified pattern). Strong names survive: run keeps 7 (documented+tested+code-mentioned).
decisions.alternatives: no penalty (218 selected, plumbing included); hand-listing names in boilerplate_names (not codebase-agnostic)

decisions.evidence: plain \b-word matching credited English words in README prose as API mentions (+4): 'this' scored 7 and was SELECTED as an API; run/close/write/read all carried a spurious mentioned_in_docs(+4). Code-context matching (fenced block, backticks, call form) removes prose-only credit; raptorJoin/geoTiff/shapefile/overlay keep theirs (verified present in doc code blocks).

'def this(...)' is Scala auxiliary-constructor syntax, not an API name; the def-regex swept it in as a phantom API 'this' (17 sites) which then scored 7 via documented + prose-mention + non_override and was selected.
decisions.alternatives: boilerplate penalty (-4) - insufficient, still scored 3-7 depending on signals

decisions.evidence: no recorded justification. TODO: sample-size sweep {3,5,10} x repeated runs -> pass-rate variance; pick smallest n with acceptable CI (needs OPENAI_API_KEY).
kept off so it stays A/B-able; targets the ~53% receiver/entry-point failure class. TODO: on/off comparison on the same sampled APIs (needs OPENAI_API_KEY).

docs/LLM_readme.md is currently 552,866 chars (~138K tokens estimated at chars/4), generated from the OLD selected surface. Regenerating from the deduped 205-name surface (203 catalog entries after full-forwarder aliasing; 418 def sites collapsed to 205 canonical entries + variants) is the token payoff; measure before/after tokens on regen.
decisions.status: pending: run `aideal readme --generate` (or catalogue regen) on a machine with OPENAI_API_KEY, then re-run comprehension to confirm pass rate does not drop

decisions.value: same-file param-type-prefix overloads collapse into the longest form; canonical election = non-deprioritized path > documented > longest params; scala-spark adapter deprioritizes Java*.scala facades
decisions.evidence: RDPro: 19/418 selected def sites are provably telescoping convenience forms (17 names). Without deprioritize_paths the canonical for geoTiff elected JavaSpatialSparkContext.geoTiff(filename,layer,opts): JavaRasterRDD - the Java-typed facade the pinned preamble deliberately avoids (it forced invented .toRDD adapters, see configs/aideal.yaml preamble note). With it, canonicals land on RaptorMixin.geoTiff / RaptorMixin.raptorJoin / ReadWriteMixin.shapefile - exactly the verified call forms in logs/error_log.jsonl passing rows.
decisions.alternatives: longest-params only (promotes Java facades); documented-first only (both contexts documented, does not discriminate)
